slides.text: Have your child trace the letter multiple times, emphasizing the /h/ sound
slides.mascot_explanation: Now it's your turn! Feel how the sandpaper letter is bumpy and rough. Trace it slowly and remember to make that gentle /h/ sound like you're breathing out.

slides.prompt: Play 'I Spy' with /h/ sound objects, then sort items into /h/ and non-/h/ sound groups
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's play detective and find things that start with the /h/ sound! Listen carefully to each word and decide if it starts like 'hat' or 'horse'.

items: Can you trace the letter H and make its sound?
Which of these objects starts with /h/: hat or book?
Can you find something in the room that starts with /h/?
slides.mascot_explanation: You're doing such a great job learning about the letter H! Let's see how well you can remember its sound and shape.

slides.text: Practice air writing the letter H, then teach the sound to a favorite toy or stuffed animal
slides.mastery_check: Child can trace letter H correctly and identify /h/ sound in simple words
slides.mascot_explanation: What a wonderful H expert you've become! Now you can teach your teddy bear or favorite toy all about the /h/ sound too.

title: Number Rods and Numerals 1-10: Complete Matching and Counting Review
description: Children review the complete set of number rods 1-10 while practicing numeral recognition and one-to-one correspondence. This comprehensive review strengthens understanding of quantity and symbol relationships.
instructions: 1. Lay out number rods 1-10 in random order on floor or large mat
2. Have child arrange rods in order from shortest to longest
3. Count each rod section together, emphasizing one-to-one correspondence
4. Introduce sandpaper numerals 1-10 by tracing and naming each
5. Match each numeral card to its corresponding number rod
6. Practice counting objects and matching to correct numeral card
7. Play 'find the number' game with numerals 1-10
8. Review by having child teach numbers to stuffed animal
materials_needed: Number rods 1-10 (or substitute with colored blocks/strips)
parent_notes: This lesson consolidates the full year's work with number rods and numerals. Allow plenty of time for hands-on manipulation - the physical experience of length differences reinforces abstract number concepts. If your child struggles with all ten numbers, focus on 1-5 first. The goal is solid understanding of quantity-to-symbol relationships, which forms the foundation for all future math learning.

slides.heading: Number Rods and Numerals 1-10: Complete Matching and Counting Review
slides.subheading: Mastering quantities and number symbols together
slides.mascot_explanation: Hi mathematicians! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going to work with ALL the numbers from 1 to 10! We'll use our special rods and number cards to become counting experts.

items: Number rods 1-10 (or substitute with colored blocks/strips)
slides.setup_instructions: Clear a large floor space or use a big mat where the number rods can be arranged comfortably.
slides.mascot_explanation: Look at all our wonderful number materials! We have long and short rods, special bumpy number cards, and lots of little things to count.

slides.text: Lay out number rods 1-10 in random order on floor or large mat
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's start by spreading out all our number rods! They're all mixed up right now - some long ones, some short ones, just like a puzzle waiting to be solved.

slides.text: Have child arrange rods in order from shortest to longest
slides.mascot_explanation: Now comes the fun part - can you put these rods in order like stairs? Start with the tiniest one and build up to the biggest one!

slides.prompt: Match numeral cards to corresponding number rods, then practice counting objects and finding the right numeral
slides.mascot_explanation: You're doing such careful work matching the number cards to the rods! Each rod shows us how many, and each card shows us the number symbol.

items: Can you count the sections on the number 7 rod?
Which numeral card matches the longest rod?
Can you find 4 counting objects and show me the numeral 4?
slides.mascot_explanation: You've learned so much about numbers! Let's make sure you can count, match, and find all these different numbers like a true math expert.

slides.text: Practice 'teaching' the numbers 1-10 to a favorite stuffed animal, demonstrating counting and matching
slides.mastery_check: Child can arrange number rods in order and match at least 5 numerals correctly to quantities
slides.mascot_explanation: What an amazing number teacher you are! Your stuffed animal is so lucky to learn about counting from such a skilled mathematician like you.

title: Playdough Sculpture: Creating Animals and Nature Forms
description: Children explore three-dimensional art through playdough manipulation, creating animals and nature-inspired sculptures. This activity develops fine motor skills, creativity, and spatial awareness through tactile artistic expression.
instructions: 1. Set up workspace with playdough, tools, and inspiration pictures of animals
2. Demonstrate basic playdough techniques: rolling, pinching, pressing, pulling
3. Show how to create simple animal forms starting with basic shapes
4. Encourage child to choose their favorite animal and begin creating
5. Support their creative process without directing the outcome
6. Invite child to add details like texture, eyes, or other features
7. Create a simple scene or environment for their animal sculpture
8. Display finished work and discuss the creative choices made
materials_needed: Simple sculpting tools (plastic knife, fork, rolling pin)
parent_notes: Playdough work strengthens hand muscles essential for writing while encouraging creative expression. Resist the urge to 'fix' or perfect your child's creations - the process and their satisfaction with their work is more important than realistic results. This medium allows for endless revision and experimentation, building confidence in artistic expression. Focus on effort and creativity rather than outcome.

slides.heading: Playdough Sculpture: Creating Animals and Nature Forms
slides.subheading: Building three-dimensional art with our hands
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello creative friends! I'm Betsy Bear and today we get to be sculptors! We'll use our hands and playdough to create amazing animals and nature sculptures.

items: Simple sculpting tools (plastic knife, fork, rolling pin)
slides.setup_instructions: Arrange materials on a comfortable work surface where your child can move freely and explore the playdough.
slides.mascot_explanation: Look at all these wonderful colors of playdough and special tools! We can roll it, squish it, and shape it into any animal we can imagine.

slides.text: Set up workspace with playdough, tools, and inspiration pictures of animals
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's get our art studio ready! Having pictures of animals nearby can give us ideas, but remember - your sculpture can look however you want it to.

slides.text: Demonstrate basic playdough techniques: rolling, pinching, pressing, pulling
slides.mascot_explanation: Watch how I can roll the playdough into a snake, pinch it to make it skinny, or press it flat! Your hands are like magic tools for shaping.

slides.prompt: Create your favorite animal using playdough, adding details and textures as desired
slides.mascot_explanation: Now it's time to bring your animal to life! Take your time and enjoy feeling the playdough change shape in your hands. There's no wrong way to create your sculpture.

items: Can you tell me about the animal you created?
What was your favorite technique to use with the playdough?
How did you make the special details on your sculpture?
slides.mascot_explanation: I love hearing about your artistic choices! Every sculptor has their own special way of creating, and yours is wonderful.

slides.text: Display your sculpture and share what you enjoyed most about the creative process
slides.mastery_check: Child can manipulate playdough using various techniques and create recognizable forms
slides.mascot_explanation: What a beautiful sculpture you created! I'm so proud of how you used your imagination and hands to make something completely your own.

title: Harold and the Purple Crayon: Story Discussion and Creative Extension
description: Children listen to Harold and the Purple Crayon and engage in meaningful discussion about creativity and imagination. This beloved story sparks conversation about problem-solving and the power of artistic expression.
instructions: 1. Gather in a comfortable reading spot with the book and any props
2. Introduce the story and show the cover, predicting what might happen
3. Read Harold and the Purple Crayon with expression and pacing
4. Pause occasionally to discuss Harold's creative solutions
5. After reading, discuss favorite parts and Harold's adventures
6. Talk about what they would draw if they had a magic crayon
7. Optional: provide purple crayons for child to create their own adventure
8. End by connecting the story to their own creativity and imagination
materials_needed: Harold and the Purple Crayon book
parent_notes: This story beautifully demonstrates creative problem-solving and the power of imagination. Use Harold's adventures to discuss how children can use creativity to solve problems in their own lives. The simple illustrations invite children to see that their own artistic expressions are valuable. Encourage open-ended discussion rather than comprehension questions - let their natural curiosity guide the conversation.

slides.heading: Harold and the Purple Crayon: Story Discussion and Creative Extension
slides.subheading: Exploring imagination and creative problem-solving
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello story lovers! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going on an amazing adventure with Harold! He has a very special purple crayon that can create anything he imagines.

items: Harold and the Purple Crayon book
slides.setup_instructions: Create a cozy reading nook where you can both see the book clearly and have space for discussion.
slides.mascot_explanation: Look! We have Harold's special book and even our own purple crayons! After we read together, you might want to create your own magical adventures.

slides.text: Gather in a comfortable reading spot with the book and any props
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's make our reading spot extra cozy! When we're comfortable, we can really focus on Harold's amazing journey and use our imagination along with him.

slides.text: Introduce the story and show the cover, predicting what might happen
slides.mascot_explanation: Look at this cover! What do you think Harold might do with that purple crayon? Making guesses before we read makes stories even more exciting!

slides.prompt: Listen to the story, then discuss Harold's creative solutions and what you would draw with a magic crayon
slides.mascot_explanation: Harold is so creative with his crayon! As we read, think about all the clever ways he solves problems by drawing exactly what he needs.

items: What was your favorite thing Harold drew?
How did Harold solve problems in the story?
If you had a magic crayon, what would you draw first?
slides.mascot_explanation: You have such wonderful ideas! I love hearing about which parts of Harold's adventure excited you most and what you would create.

slides.text: Optional drawing time with purple crayon, creating your own Harold-inspired adventure
slides.mastery_check: Child can retell key story events and express personal connections to Harold's creativity
slides.mascot_explanation: Just like Harold, you have amazing imagination and creativity inside you! Every time you draw or create something, you're going on your own special adventure.
